title: 1.1: Reports how the backend is started in local development mode (e.g., dev command used or process entry point observed at runtime).
1.2: Reports how configuration is loaded during startup (e.g., environment variables, config files, or runtime-loaded settings observed in logs).
1.3: Reports log messages indicating the application is ready to accept requests (e.g., server listening or ready-state log output).
1.4: Reports the network port exposed by the backend at runtime (e.g., port 7777 as configured in local_main).
1.5: Reports endpoints exposed after startup (e.g., /health, auth routes, API routes).
1.6: Reports where an incoming request first enters the application (e.g., Flask app, before_request hooks, or middleware entry point).
1.7: Reports how user identity is determined during request handling (e.g., Flask-Login user_loader, session-based resolution, or API key via Authentication header).
1.8: Reports how authentication context is carried through request processing (e.g., current_user proxy, g.user, or session-backed identity across handlers).
1.9: Reports whether background jobs, schedulers, or workers start automatically at startup, including confirming their absence if none start.
2.1: Claims that the Flask development server binds to all network interfaces (0.0.0.0) making it externally accessible.
